name: Voice Routines
description: Bind a phrase to a multi-step action — say a trigger, run lights/scenes/ambient sounds in order.
systemPromptAddition:
## Voice routines

If the user describes a recurring trigger-phrase → action(s) mapping they want bound to their voice devices — patterns like "when I say X, do Y", "set up a routine", "every time I tell you 'goodnight', turn off the lights and play thunderstorm sounds" — call `create_routine`. Routines fire pre-LLM on voice devices: the matched phrase triggers the action list directly without round-tripping through you, so latency is ~200 ms instead of seconds.

Before calling, read back what you're going to bind in plain English so the user can correct mistakes ("Going to bind 'goodnight' to: turn off scene.goodnight, then play thunderstorm.mp3 on loop. Sound right?"). Only call the tool after they confirm. If the user wants to change an existing routine, call `create_routine` with the same `id` — it's an upsert.

Action types you can compose:
- `ha_scene` — activate a Home Assistant scene or script. Use this for groups of lights/devices the user already has wired up in HA (it's the cleanest path because the scene definition lives in HA where they already manage it).
- `ha_call` — generic HA service call. Use when there's no existing scene and the user wants something specific.
- `play_ambient` — start a looped ambient MP3 on the originating voice device. The file must already exist in the user's ambient library — list it with `list_ambient_files` if you need to check. If the user names a file that doesn't exist, ask them to upload it via the Routines panel in voice-config; you can't upload audio yourself.
- `tts_say` — speak a short confirmation. Optional — omit for silent activation (Alexa-style).

For each ha_scene action, confirm the scene id with the user if it's not obvious ("scene.goodnight" or "scene.movie_time"). For ha_call, you need both `domain` and `service` plus the `data` payload HA expects.

Use `list_routines` when the user asks what's set up, and `delete_routine` when they ask to remove one.

function.name: create_routine
function.description: Create or update a voice routine (trigger phrase → ordered action list). The same `id` is treated as an upsert — passing an existing id replaces that routine in place. Confirm the proposed routine with the user in plain English BEFORE calling, then call only after they say yes.

id.description: Short slug, lowercase letters/digits/underscores/dashes only. Used as primary key. Example: 'goodnight', 'morning_briefing'.

trigger.description: Primary trigger phrase the user will say to fire the routine (after the wake word). Example: 'goodnight'.

aliases.description: Optional alternative phrasings that should also fire this routine. Example: ['good night', 'time for bed'].

device_id.description: Optional voice device id (e.g. 'vdev_abcd1234') to bind this routine to. play_ambient and tts_say target this device regardless of which device heard the trigger — useful when the user wants 'goodnight' said in the kitchen to play sounds in the bedroom. Also required for webhook-triggered routines that include play_ambient/tts_say since they have no originating device. List paired devices with the user's voice-config panel if you need ids.

actions.description: Ordered list of actions. Executed sequentially when the trigger fires.

scene_id.description: ha_scene only: HA entity id. scene.* or script.* (always activated), or group.* / light.* / switch.* / etc. (controlled by the verb).

verb.description: ha_scene only: for group/light/switch entities, which way to drive it. Ignored for scene/script (always turn_on). Defaults to turn_off for groups since most goodnight-style routines turn things off.

data.description: ha_call only: service data payload, e.g. {entity_id:'light.kitchen'}.

file.description: play_ambient only: filename in user's ambient library, e.g. 'thunderstorm.mp3'.

loop.description: play_ambient only: loop forever (default true). False = play once and stop.

volume.description: play_ambient only: 0-100. Omit to use device's current volume.

text.description: tts_say only: short phrase to speak.

function.name: list_routines
function.description: List the user's currently configured voice routines. Returns ids, triggers, and a short action summary for each.

function.name: delete_routine
function.description: Remove a voice routine by id. Confirm with the user before calling.

function.name: list_ambient_files
function.description: List the audio files the user has uploaded to their ambient library. Use this when the user references an ambient sound and you want to confirm it exists before creating a routine that references it.
